## Core requirement
No incoherent intermediate frame may reach the user.

## Primary goals
- eliminate stretched/invalid transient visuals
- maintain visual continuity during reflow convergence
- minimize work through dirty-region updates
- stabilize frame pacing with hitch accounting

## Compatibility requirement
GPU acceleration is preferred, but CPU fallback must preserve behavior and pass equivalence checks.

## Core requirement
Aggressive optimization must not compromise correctness or availability.

## Required capabilities
- explicit invariants
- fuzz/property assault on state transitions
- stall watchdogs
- crash forensics payloads
- degradation ladder with clear triggers/recovery

## Guiding principle
Degrade quality before correctness; degrade correctness only as a last resort to preserve interaction continuity.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.7.1","title":"Expand module-level unit tests for screen/mux/layout/render resize semantics","description":"Add exhaustive unit tests for core components and explicit edge-case coverage. Ensure failures include diagnostic context useful for rapid triage.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.7.4","title":"Implement performance regression suite with strict CI quality gates","description":"## Context
Performance regressions must be blocked automatically before merge and before release promotion.

## Scope
Implement benchmark and e2e performance gates for resize/reflow behavior, including artifact and interaction-latency checks where applicable.

- Baseline management workflow (refresh policy + audit trail)
- Regression classification output (hard fail vs warning with rationale)
- Machine-readable reports for frame pacing, interaction latency, and resource usage

## Why this matters
Objective automated gates prevent subjective performance regressions from reaching users.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails on threshold violations with actionable diagnostics.
- Baseline drift is controlled via explicit approval workflow.
- Reports include p50/p95/p99 and scenario-level breakdowns.
- Gate definitions map back to SLO bead outputs (`wa-1u90p.1.4`).

- Attach machine-readable artifacts (trace bundle, frame histogram, failure signature) for every failing case.

+{"id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","title":"Add interactive input-latency e2e harness during resize storms (typing/mouse/paste)","description":"## Context
Users judge responsiveness by how quickly input is reflected while resizing.

## Scope
Create e2e harness that injects typing, mouse, and paste events during high-frequency resize and font changes, measuring end-to-end interaction latency.

## Deliverables
- Input latency measurement scripts and thresholds
- Per-event timeline logs with causality IDs
- Automated regression reports for p50/p95/p99 interaction lag

## Why this matters
Ensures optimization effort improves felt responsiveness, not only backend timings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Harness captures event-to-visible-effect latency with millisecond resolution.
- Regression gates fail on latency budget violations under stress scenarios.
- Logs include scheduler decisions and queue state needed for root-cause analysis.

## Logging Requirements
- Emit structured JSONL logs with correlation identifiers: resize_transaction_id, pane_id, tab_id, sequence_no, scheduler_decision, frame_id, and test_case_id.
- Emit per-stage timing fields (queue_wait_ms, reflow_ms, render_ms, present_ms) plus aggregate p50/p95/p99.
- Attach machine-readable artifacts (trace bundle, frame histogram, failure signature) for every failing case.

## Coverage Expectations

- Include explicit assertions for user-visible behavior, not only internal counters.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:30.178188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:22:42.506631Z","labels":["e2e","input-latency","reflow","resize","testing","track-validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:30.178188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:39.379193Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:39.479994Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:39.582381Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:39.682369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.7.8","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.7.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:17:43.054721Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","title":"Define no-regression compatibility contract for terminal semantics and user workflows","description":"## Context
Resize improvements are only successful if terminal semantics and common workflows remain correct.

## Scope
Define the no-regression compatibility contract as release policy, covering cursor behavior, wrapping semantics, scrollback integrity, alt-screen behavior, and interaction continuity.

## Deliverables
- Compatibility contract document with explicit invariant list
- Mapping from invariants to validation suites and artifact evidence
- Escalation process when contract checks fail

## Why this matters
This protects users from subtle behavioral regressions masked by performance gains.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contract enumerates all critical user-visible semantics and invariants.
- Each invariant is mapped to at least one automated test/e2e assertion.
- Alt-screen and interaction-continuity checks are explicitly included.
- Contract is referenced by rollout and final go/no-go decision beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.126624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:53:49.899497Z","labels":["compatibility","reflow","resize","track-rollout","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:48.126624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.236341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.340057Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:37.443933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
